commit.message:
feat: 30+ reactive hooks, router crate, comprehensive API docs

New reactive utilities (oxide-core):
  - watch(source, callback) — react to signal changes, skips initial
  - on_mount(f) — run after current microtask completes
  - on_cleanup(f) — register cleanup for beforeunload

New reactive hooks (oxide-dom):
  - use_local_storage(key, default) — persistent signal
  - use_interval(ms) — reactive timer tick
  - use_debounce(signal, ms) — debounced output signal
  - use_throttle(signal, ms) — throttled output signal
  - use_window_size() — reactive (width, height)
  - use_scroll() — reactive document scroll position
  - use_mouse() — reactive global mouse position
  - use_media_query(query) — reactive CSS media query
  - use_online() — network online/offline status
  - use_preferred_dark() — prefers-color-scheme signal
  - use_click_outside(el, handler) — click outside detection
  - use_focus(el) — focus tracking signal
  - set_title(title) — set document title
  - set_meta(name, content) — set/create meta tags

New crate: oxide-router — client-side routing:
  - Router::new(mode, routes) — hash or history mode
  - route(path, view) — define routes with :param segments
  - router.view() — reactive outlet that swaps views
  - navigate(path) — programmatic navigation
  - link(href, text) / nav_link() — client-side links
  - use_route() — current path signal
  - use_param(name) / use_params() — route parameters

Docs rewrite (docs.html):
  - 16 categorized sections with table of contents
  - ~90 APIs documented with code examples
  - Categories: Getting Started, CLI, Core Reactivity, View Macro,
    Reactive Hooks, DOM Utils, Head Management, Routing, Timers,
    Storage, Console, Location, OTel, Resiliency, Components, Debugging

Framework now has 9 crates, ~90 public APIs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Malgo 2025 migration (phase 4 + parser unification) (#284)

* feat(ir): extend IR with Fix, Mu, Cocase, Destructor, ExternalCall, BinOp, Ifz

Add new constructors to all IR layers (Fun, Full, Flat, Join):
- Fun: Fix (fixed point operator)
- Producer: Mu (continuation capture), Cocase (codata construction)
- Consumer: Destructor (destructor application)
- Statement: ExternalCall, BinOp, Ifz (conditional on zero)

Each constructor includes all required instances (Store, ToSExpr, HasRange).
FlatPass and JoinPass handlers added for new constructors.
ToCore stubs added for Fix translation.
Eval stubs added for new constructors + new builtin string functions
(strLen, strAt, strSub, strToInt, intToStr, runeToStr, intToRune, runeToInt).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(runtime): extend runtime, examples, and test infrastructure

- Builtin.mlg: add modulo (Int32/Int64), negation (Int32/Int64/Float/Double),
  chr (Int32->Char), and string reverse foreign imports with boxed wrappers
- Prelude.mlg: add list utilities (null, length, reverse, filter, zip, zipWith,
  replicate, any, all, foldr, append, concatList, take, drop) and numeric
  utilities (abs, max, min)
- New examples: FizzBuzz, BinaryTree (BST), Collatz sequence, HigherOrder
  (compose, twice, map/filter/fold/zip pipeline)
- New test cases: ArithInt32, HigherOrder, ListOps, MapFilter, StringOps

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(elaborate): add ElaboratePass for copattern transformation

Add a new compiler pass that transforms copattern definitions (codata
expressions) into records and lambdas at the surface syntax level,
before conversion to Fun IR.

Algorithm based on ziku's Elaborate implementation:
- Field copatterns → Record expressions
- Apply copatterns → Lambda (Fn) expressions with pattern matching
- Mixed accessor kinds are rejected with descriptive errors
- Uses Temporal IDs for hygienic name generation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(backend): add Scheme code generation backend

Add a new Scheme backend that translates Join IR to Chez Scheme source
code. Includes identifier mangling, string escaping, primitive operation
compilation, pattern matching, and a minimal runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(features): add Malgo2025 feature flag

Add Malgo2025 constructor to the Feature type with pragma parsing
support (#malgo-2025) and isMalgo2025Enabled utility function.
This flag gates Malgo 2025 language features (copatterns, label/goto,
variant types, etc.) behind an opt-in pragma.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(syntax): add label/goto, bottom type, tilde type, and row polymorphism

Add new syntax constructors and parsing for:
- Label/Goto expressions for control flow with labels
- Bottom type (_|_) for non-terminating expressions
- Tilde type (~T) for covalue/continuation types
- Variant type ([Cons1 Int | Cons2 | r]) with row polymorphism
- Row polymorphism for record types ({x: Int | r})

Extension.hs: Add XLabel, XGoto, XTyBottom, XTyTilde, XTyVariant type families
Syntax.hs: Add Label, Goto, TyBottom, TyTilde, TyVariant constructors; update TyRecord with row tail
Parser: Add parsers for new constructs in both Regular and CStyle variants
Rename/Pass.hs: Add stubs for new constructors, update getTyVars and rnType
ToFun.hs: Add stubs for Label and Goto translation

Also fix compilation errors in other units' code:
- Infer: update TyRecord pattern match, fix imports
- BigStepEval: fix fromJust usage
- InferSpec: fix runPureEff, isRight, Set.fromList
- SchemeSpec: fix Text/String type mismatch

commit.message:
fix: replace full-response hash with coarse structural token hash

The full normalized-response hash treated every distinct QMP argument
combination as unique coverage (e.g., device-list-properties(e1000) vs
device-list-properties(rtl8139) -> different hashes despite same behavior
shape), causing 97.7% per-execution corpus add rate and 10M+ queue files
after 24h of fuzzing.

New approach: classify each QMP response line into one of ~12 stable
structural tokens (R0/RA/RO/RS for success variants, E:ClassName for
errors, V:EventName for async events). Hash the sorted multiset of tokens.
This caps practical corpus size to ~10K-50K and eliminates fine-grained
content-based false diversity. Verified: 956 entries after 500 iters.

Additional changes:
- Add thread-id: to SPACED_PATTERNS (normalizes query-cpus-fast OS PID)
- Add qmp_response_token() + find_bytes() helper functions
- Add op 17: qom-set (write QOM properties, exercises property-setter paths)
  with 10 test values: true/false/0/1/255/65535/4294967295/string/null
- Update ChunkOpMutator to below(18)
- Add seeds: seed-qom-set.bin, seed-qom-set-int.bin

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add -no-pie to BIOS link flags

Modern GCC defaults to PIE even with -fno-pic at compile time, causing
R_X86_64_32S relocation errors when linking the freestanding BIOS stage2
binary. Add -no-pie to both CMAKE_EXE_LINKER_FLAGS_INIT in the bios
toolchain and target_link_options in the bios target to force a plain
non-PIE executable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Create requireProfile middleware that checks Firestore profile existence
- Add profile field to Express Request type augmentation
- Apply requireProfile after requireAuth on POST /burn-buddies/requests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-004 - Fix G-3: Username uniqueness race condition

Replace read-check-write pattern in generateUniqueUsername() with atomic
doc.create() calls. The function now atomically reserves usernames by
attempting to create the doc (which fails with ALREADY_EXISTS if taken),
eliminating the TOCTOU race condition where concurrent profile creations
could claim the same username.

- Changed generateUniqueUsername() to accept uid parameter and use
  doc.create() for atomic reservation
- Added tryReserveUsername() helper that handles ALREADY_EXISTS (code 6)
- Updated profile creation paths in users.ts to skip batch username
  reservation (now done in generateUniqueUsername)
- Added concurrent claim test in username.test.ts
- Updated G-3 gap tests to assert fixed behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update apps/api/src/modules/calling/calling.routes.js

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace ljmp with lretq for x86_64; suppress unused-function warnings

os/windows.c: 'ljmp $seg, $off' is unsupported by GAS in 64-bit mode.
Split the #ifdef: __i386__ keeps ljmp; __x86_64__ uses the lretq trick
(push CS=0, push RIP=VBR_LOAD_ADDR, lretq) which is the 64-bit
equivalent of a far jump. Also add (void)ep at function top to silence
unused-parameter warning.

os/linux.c, common/menu.c, bios/stage2/fat.c: mark static helper
functions (str_len, print_int, is_space) with __attribute__((unused))
to silence -Wunused-function without removing them.

commit.message:
fix: Make Cosmos DB optional during API startup

The API was crashing at startup when Cosmos was not available, preventing
standalone and test scenarios from running. This fix makes Cosmos registration
conditional based on configuration availability.

Changes:
- Made CosmosClient registration conditional (checks connection strings and config)
- Only register Cosmos-dependent services when Cosmos is configured
- Added user seeding during Cosmos initialization for identity
- Added CosmosUserService implementation for user authentication
- Log clear warning when running in standalone mode without persistence

This allows the API to start successfully in all environments:
- With Aspire: Full Cosmos initialization and seeding runs
- Without Aspire: API starts with warning, no data persistence
- Tests can now reach the API without requiring full orchestration

Fixes #44

commit.message:
feat: replace Nominatim text geocoding with Azure OpenAI

New 3-step geocoding pipeline:
1. EXIF GPS  → Nominatim reverse geocode (kept — GPS is authoritative)
2. AOAI text → gpt-4o-mini extracts precise coordinates from message text
              (handles landmarks, restaurants, natural language context)
3. AOAI vision → gpt-4o-mini image recognition as last resort before giving up

Azure OpenAI resource: munchhatmap-aoai-prod (eastus, S0, Standard)
Model: gpt-4o-mini (10K TPM) — cheapest vision+text capable model

Changes:
- infra/modules/openai.bicep: new module, gpt-4o-mini Standard deployment
- infra/main.bicep: wire openai module, add openAiLocation param + output
- infra/modules/keyvault.bicep: aoai-endpoint and aoai-key placeholder secrets
- infra/modules/containerapp.bicep: AZURE_OPENAI_* env vars via KV secretRef
- bot/src/handlers/aoai.ts: geocodeWithText() and geocodeWithImage() via AzureOpenAI client
- bot/src/handlers/pinProcessor.ts: new 3-step flow, remove Nominatim text search
- bot/package.json: add openai ^6.27.0
- .env.example: document AZURE_OPENAI_* vars

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: hoist § markers from Lisp expressions and fix empty ASSIGN for collections (#550)

- Hoist §-containing operands in binary/unary operations to temp vars,
  preventing § section markers inside Lisp (op arg1 arg2) expressions
  that cause Calor0114 parse errors
- Fix empty §ASSIGN when RHS is a collection creation (List, Dict, Set,
  Array, MultiDimArray) by emitting the collection block with the
  assignment target name directly
- Add 8 new unit tests for hoisting and collection assignment patterns

Results on real-world codebases:
  Newtonsoft.Json: 239/239 = 100% (was 173/240 = 72.1%)
  Humanizer sample: 99/100 = 99% (was ~86.1%)
